explanation: This command uses the original flag format and a valid path, but normalizes the path and uses escaped quotes to test the Sigma rule detection.
This command omits quotes around the path, which is still valid in many command-line contexts and tests if the rule detects the flag without additional syntax.
This command uses the long path prefix (\?), which is a valid Windows path normalization technique and tests if the rule handles extended path syntax.
This command uses escaped backslashes and quotes, which are still parsed correctly by the command line and test the Sigma rule's parsing robustness.
This command uses an environment variable to represent part of the path, testing if the rule can detect the flag when paths are dynamically resolved.
